title: Fix scales and chord charts not displaying for manual and locked-in keys

requirements.text: Ensure that when a key is set manually via the KeySelector component, the TheoryPanel component receives and correctly renders the selected key's chord progressions, scales, and other music theory content.

quotes: chord charts and scales and stuff never show up when I set the key manually

acceptanceCriteria: When a user selects a note and mode from the KeySelector dropdowns and confirms the selection, the TheoryPanel immediately displays chord progressions and scales for that key.
The TheoryPanel does not remain empty or show a loading state indefinitely after a manual key selection.
Chord charts and scale information are visually rendered in the TheoryPanel for any manually selected key.

requirements.text: Ensure that when the key is auto-detected and locked in via the AudioAnalyzer component, the TheoryPanel component receives the locked key and correctly renders its chord progressions, scales, and music theory content.

quotes: I don't think that they do properly even when the key is locked in

acceptanceCriteria: When the AudioAnalyzer transitions to the 'locked' state with a detected key, the TheoryPanel displays chord progressions and scales for that locked key.
The TheoryPanel updates its content whenever the locked key changes.
Chord charts and scale information remain visible and do not disappear after the key is locked in.

requirements.text: Audit the data flow between MusicTheoryApp, KeySelector, AudioAnalyzer, and TheoryPanel to identify and fix any state management issues (e.g., incorrect prop passing, stale state, missing re-renders, or query key mismatches) that prevent the TheoryPanel from receiving and displaying the active key's music theory data.

quotes: make sure that the scales and chords actually do show up

acceptanceCriteria: The active key state (from both manual selection and auto-lock) is consistently passed to TheoryPanel as a prop or shared state.
TheoryPanel's React Query hooks receive the correct key argument and fetch data whenever the key changes.
No console errors related to undefined key values or failed data fetches occur during key selection or lock-in.

constraints: Do not modify files listed in frontend.immutablePaths.
Do not change the backend Motoko actor — it already serves the required music theory data.
nonGoals: Adding new music theory content or categories beyond what the backend already provides.
Changing the visual design or layout of the TheoryPanel or KeySelector components.
Modifying the PitchDisplay component or its placeholder logic.
